answer: The total amount of term debt reported by Apple as of September 28, 2024 is $96,662 million. [Source: Apple 10-K, Item 8 Note 9, p.394]
The Apple 10-K report for fiscal year ended September 28, 2024 was signed on November 1, 2024 and filed with the SEC on the same day. [Source: Apple 10-K, Signature Page, p.118]
According to Source 1, Apple has no unresolved staff comments from the SEC. [Apple 10-K, Item 1B, p.176]

answer: According to Source 3, Tesla's total revenue for the year ended December 31, 2023 was $96,773 million. [Source 3: Tesla 10-K, Item 7, p.51]
Approximately 84.7 percent of Tesla's total revenue in 2023 came from Automotive Sales (excluding Leasing). [Source: Tesla 10-K, Item 7, p.51]

Tesla states that it is highly dependent on Elon Musk, the Chief Executive Officer, because he is central to Tesla's strategy and operations, and his loss or inability to dedicate sufficient time could disrupt operations and harm the business. [Source: Tesla 10-K, Item 1A Risk Factors, p.22]

Tesla currently produces and delivers Model S (sedan), Model X (SUV), Model 3 (sedan), Model Y (SUV/crossover), and Cybertruck (pickup truck) [Source 1]. The Tesla Semi is in limited/pilot production [Source 1].

Tesla's lease pass-through fund arrangements allow the company to finance the cost of solar energy systems by entering into lease agreements with customers and power purchase agreements (PPAs) with investors who provide upfront capital and receive tax credits and depreciation benefits. This enables Tesla to fund solar deployments without using its own capital. [Source: Tesla 10-K, Item 7 Lease Pass-Through, p.56]
